name: Operational Reframing and Approval-Framed Delegation in Multi-Agent LLM Safety (2607.07097)

digest_notes: Safety evaluations of multi-agent LLM systems often compare a direct prompt with a planner-executor pipeline. We argue this aggregate conflates three mechanisms: harmful intent reframing, planner refusal, and approval-framed delegation. We introduce a five-condition controlled contrast design evaluated on 30 synthetic harmful scenarios. Results show operational reframing is the most portable risk signal, increasing compliance for GPT, Gemini, and DeepSeek. These findings suggest multi-agent safe
